data.title: GreenPower receives determination letter from Nasdaq
data.description:
• GreenPower Motor (NASDAQ:GP) announced on Friday that the company received a Nasdaq determination letter stating that the company has not regained compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rules.
  • The Letter stated that, unless the company requests an appeal of this determination on September 3, 2025, the staff has determined that the company's common shares will be scheduled for delisting from Nasdaq and will be suspended at the opening of business on September 5, 2025, and a Form 25-NSE will be filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission which will remove the company's common shares from listing and registration on Nasdaq.
  • The company intends to request a hearing before the Panel to appeal the Letter. A hearing request will stay the suspension of the company's common shares and the filing of the Form 25-NSE pending the Panel's decision.
  • GP -1.47% after hours to $0.335.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: WNS shareholders approve Capgemini acquisition with overwhelming majority
data.description:
• WNS (Holdings) (NYSE:WNS) said its shareholders overwhelmingly approved the proposed acquisition by Capgemini SE (EUR: CAP) at both the Court Meeting and General Meeting held on August 29, 2025.
  • Approximately 99.9% of shares voted were in favor, representing ~79.2% of WNS' total outstanding shares as of the record date.
  • The $76.50 per share all-cash transaction, announced on July 7, 2025, values WNS at $3.3 billion, excluding net financial debt.
  • The deal is expected to close before year-end, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals.
  • “We are pleased with the results of today’s Court Meeting and General Meeting and thank our shareholders for their strong support of this transformative combination,” WNS CEO, Murugesh said in a statement.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nasdaq slides 1% as chip stocks weigh, with Wall Street posting a marginal weekly loss
data.description:
Wall Street on Friday fell, weighed down by a slide in semiconductor stocks. Market participants also received a key in-line inflation reading that did little to change expectations of a Federal Reserve interest rate cut in September.

The negative session contributed to overall slight weekly losses for all three major averages. 

The benchmark S&P 500 gauge (SP500) fell -0.6%, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) slipped -0.2%. 

The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) slid -1.2%. Marvell Technology (MRVL) slumped -18.6% and ended as the top percentage loser on the tech-heavy index after the chipmaker's quarterly report disappointed investors. Other chip stocks also came under pressure. 

Meanwhile, Nvidia (NVDA), the world's largest chipmaker, declined -3.3%. A report said Chinese tech giant Alibaba (BABA) had developed a new artificial intelligence (AI) chip to help fill in the absence left by Nvidia (NVDA) in the Asian market.

Also weighing on markets was Dell Technologies (DELL). The stock was -8.9% amid concerns over the PC and electronic maker's quarterly AI server margins.

“Given all the blood pressure medication taken in anticipation of Nvidia’s earnings this week, it was almost written in the stars that the week should close more or less flat for each of the major indices. The market now heads into a seasonally weak month; that said, since April the only surprises have been to the upside, so trading the calendar seems unwise; price will tell us where price is going," Alex King, investing group leader of Cestrian Capital Research, told Seeking Alpha.   

A busy economic calendar was the focus on Friday, with the spotlight on the July core personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index. Widely regarded as the Fed's preferred inflation gauge, the core PCE deflator rose +0.3 M/M, flat from June and matching the consensus estimate. On a Y/Y basis, it rose +2.9%, also in line with consensus but ticking up from June's +2.8% reading.   

The largely tame inflationary signal keeps the Fed on track for an interest rate cut in September, according to the CME FedWatch tool. 

Speaking of the Fed, an emergency court hearing over Fed Governor Lisa Cook's lawsuit challenging President Trump’s attempt to remove her from the board ended with no ruling on Friday.  

Turning to the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields were mixed. Longer-term maturities gained, while shorter-term instruments slipped, keeping the yield curve steep. The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) was last up 3 basis points to 4.23%, while the more rate-sensitive 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 1 basis point to 3.63%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

data.title: Spirit Aviation shares fall more than 40% as it files for Chapter 11
data.description:
Spirit Aviation (NYSE:FLYY), the parent company of Spirit Airlines said on Friday that it is executing a comprehensive restructuring of the airline. 

To facilitate the process, the company has filed voluntary petitions for Chapter 11 in the U.S. Bankruptcy Court for the Southern District of New York. 

Spirit intends to use the Chapter 11 process to implement broad changes to the company. The company has been actively engaged with certain of its largest lessors, secured noteholders and key stakeholders over the past few months as it works to refine its path forward. 

The Chapter 11 process will provide Spirit the tools, time and flexibility to continue ongoing discussions with all of its lessors, financial creditors and other parties. 

The company is filing customary motions with the Court to enable it to conduct business as normal during the restructuring process. 

Spirit expects to be delisted from the NYSE American Stock Exchange in the near term as a result of the Chapter 11 filing, and the company expects that its common stock will continue to trade in the over-the-counter marketplace through the Chapter 11 process. T

The shares are expected to be cancelled and have no value as part of Spirit's restructuring.

Spirit Aviation (NYSE:FLYY) shares were down more than 45% in extended trading.

data.title: Avista receives approval of all-party, all issues settlement in Idaho general rate cases
data.description:
• Avista (NYSE:AVA) has received approval from the Idaho Public Utilities Commission of the all-party, all issues settlement agreement that was filed on June 9, 2025, concluding the company’s electric and natural gas general rate cases, the company said on Friday.
  • New electric rates take effect Sept. 1, 2025 and Sept 1, 2026.
  • The approved rates are designed to increase annual base electric revenues by $19.5 million or 6.3%, effective Sept. 1, 2025, and by $14.7 million or 4.5%, effective Sept. 1, 2026.
  • For natural gas, the settlement agreement is designed to increase annual base natural gas revenues by $4.6 million or 9.2%, effective Sept. 1, 2025, and reduce base revenues by $0.2 million or 0.4%, effective Sept. 1, 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ScanTech AI receives Nasdaq deficiency letter
data.description:
• ScanTech AI Systems (NASDAQ:STAI) announced on Friday that the company received a Nasdaq letter, stating that the company is not in compliance with the Nasdaq Listing Rule as a result of not having timely filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission its quarterly report on Form 10-Q for the quarterly period ended June 30, 2025. 
  • The company said the notice has no immediate effect on the listing of the company’s common stock on Nasdaq.
  • The notice stated that the company has 60 calendar days to submit a plan to regain compliance. 
  • STAI +1.93% after hours to $0.431.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Jacobio Pharmaceuticals Group reports 1H results
data.description:
• Jacobio Pharmaceuticals Group press release (OTCPK:JBPHF): 1H Revenue of RMB45.7M.
  • The net loss for the first half of 2025 was RMB59.0 million,  narrowing by 65.1% compared with the same period in 2024.

data.title: SU Group Holdings receives Nasdaq notice for public float deficiency
data.description:
• SU Group Holdings (NASDAQ:SUGP) on Friday said it received a deficiency notice from Nasdaq on Aug. 27 for failing to maintain the minimum number of publicly held shares required under Listing Rule 5550(a)(4).
  • Nasdaq determined the company has ~424,000 publicly held shares as of August 25, below the 500,000 minimum threshold. The figure excludes shares held by officers, directors, or 10%+ beneficial owners.
  • The notice has no immediate impact on the company’s Nasdaq listing or trading, which continues under the ticker "SUGP."
  • SU Group has until October 13 to submit a compliance plan and intends to do so within the deadline.
  • SUGP -0.71% after hours to $6.25.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: U.S. drilling rig count edges lower in latest Baker Hughes report
data.description:
The number of total oil and gas drilling rigs in the U.S. fell for the 16th time in 18 weeks, declining by 2 to 536, Baker Hughes said Friday in its latest report.

Oil rigs rose by 1 to 412 during the week, while gas rigs lost 3 to 119 and 5 rigs remained classified as miscellaneous.

The total rig count was down 47 rigs, or 8% compared to the same time last year.

Rigs targeting crude oil in the Permian Basin finished flat at 253, the count in the Eagle Ford gained 1 to 29, and the Williston Basin lost 1 to 28.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)
